matches.x: 2 Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers ?.
2 Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers ?.
He begins with "all ye old men." and ends with "you little children who can understand." - the whole society

CHILDREN OF THE THIRD GENERATION. In view of the fact that bene (children of) is in the construct with shilleshim (third), the children referred to are the fourth generation.11The children of the third generation are the fourth generation.

CHILDREN OF THE THIRD GENERATION. In view of the fact that bene (children of) is in the construct with shilleshim (third), the children referred to are the fourth generation.11The children of the third generation are the fourth generation.

"What the chewing locust left, the swarming locust has eaten; what the swarming locust left, the crawling locust has eaten; and what the crawling locust left, the consuming locust has eaten. For a nation has come up against My land, strong, and without number; his teeth are the teeth of a lion, and he has the fangs of a fierce lion" (Joel 1:4, 6).
2 Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers ?.

These are actually wicked men and armies with power to torment all of humanity except those who have God's seal in their foreheads (9:4). John's imagery of locusts is similar to Joel's description of the locust-like armies that will "come up upon my land, strong, and without number. . . . He hath laid my vine waste, and barked my fig tree: he hath made it clean bare, and cast it away; the…

2 Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers ?.

(Isa. 33:20.) The worshipful nature of these assemblies is shown in Joel's cry: "Sanctify ye a fast, call a solemn assembly, gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land into the house of the Lord your God, and cry unto the Lord." (Joel 1:14; 2:15-17.) (Bruce R. Mc Conkie, Mormon Doctrine, 2d ed., p.739)

The solemn assembly. The purpose of a solemn assembly of the Saints is for worship. Joel spoke of the nature of these assemblies: "Sanctify ye a fast, call a solemn assembly, gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land into the house of the Lord your God, and cry unto the Lord" (Joel 1:14). This revelation informed Lyman Sherman that he was to be numbered among the first to receive the…

1. In ancient Israel, as part of their Mosaic worship, frequent solemn assemblies were held. (Lev. 23:36; Num. 29:35; Deut. 16:8; 2 Chron. 7:9.) They were held in connection with their sacrifices and feasts. (Neh. 8:18; Isa. 1:10-14; Ezek. 45:17; 46:11.) Jerusalem was the city of their solemnities or set feasts. (Isa. 33:20.) The worshipful nature of these assemblies is shown in Joel's cry:…

And he had flocks of sheep, and herds of cattle, and great cultivation; and the Philistaee envied him.
He owned flocks of sheep, herds of cattle, and many slaves, and the Philistines were jealous of him.
